Featured Case Study
Apple · WWDR · 2010–2016

Helping Developers Build
Better Apple Experiences

As an App Review Specialist on Apple's Worldwide Developer Relations team, I reviewed 600,000+ iOS apps over six years — providing direct HIG-aligned UX guidance, serving on the App Review Board, and helping rejected developers successfully resubmit through the appeals process.


View Full Case Study →
600K+ Apps Reviewed
WWDR Team
Board App Review Board
6 yrs Tenure
Notable Collaboration

Worked directly with the DoorDash team to navigate App Review, providing HIG-aligned UX guidance that helped them ship successfully on the iOS App Store.

Issue 01
Navigation Misalignment

"Navigation should be predictable and consistent with platform conventions."

  • Use standard UITabBar components
  • Preserve system gesture behaviors
  • Maintain consistent hierarchy
Issue 02
Visual Hierarchy Problems

"Interfaces should communicate hierarchy through typography, spacing, and color."

  • Apply SF typography scale
  • Use 8-point spacing system
  • Reduce competing visual elements
Issue 03
Interaction Feedback

"People should always understand the result of their actions."

  • Implement visual state changes
  • Add micro-animations on interaction
  • Use system haptic feedback patterns

Full case study includes before/after analysis, redesign examples, and key takeaways from 600,000+ app reviews.
